title: What Information Does DHCP Hand Out?
choices: Default gateway and DNS server addresses

content.explanation: DHCP hands out more than just an IP address. A typical offer bundles the IP with a subnet mask, a default gateway, one or more DNS servers, a domain name, and a lease time — everything a device needs to actually talk to the network, not just have an address on it.
content.example:
ip dhcp pool OFFICE-WIFI
 network 10.20.1.0 255.255.255.0
 default-router 10.20.1.1
 dns-server 10.20.1.10
 domain-name corp.local
 lease 8

check.statement: Besides an IP address, which of these is typically included in a DHCP offer?

acceptable_answers: default gateway and dns server addresses
solution.explanation: A DHCP offer bundles the IP with the subnet mask, default gateway, and DNS server addresses (and often a domain name and lease time) — a device configured only with an IP and no gateway/DNS couldn't reach anything outside its own subnet.
